findings.description: Title block formatting is not preserved.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the document title on page 1 is centered as a compact three-line title block. In the HTML, the same text is left-aligned with larger spacing between lines, changing the visual hierarchy though not the content.

findings.description: Appendix B relationship diagram is missing as a graphic and replaced by linear text/table content.
findings.evidence: The PDF page 4 contains a visible box-and-line hierarchy diagram showing record type relationships. In the HTML, this graphic is absent; only table/text content remains, so the visual diagram is lost.

findings.description: Appendix A sample data is not preserved as structured line-based records.
findings.evidence: In the PDF on page 3, Appendix A shows many separate delimited-record lines in a monospaced/list-like layout. In the HTML, the records are collapsed into long wrapped paragraph text, making row boundaries hard to distinguish and losing the visual structure of the sample data.

findings.description: Appendix A fixed-width/sample record formatting is lost.
findings.evidence: The PDF presents the sample .txt data as clearly separated lines under the Appendix A heading. The HTML renders the data in proportional text with wrapping, which no longer resembles a delimited text-file example.

findings.description: Appendix B record-type table contains incorrect cell values and an apparent transposition of record type numbers.
findings.evidence: PDF Appendix B lists: "Record Type 8 — Fishing Gear Data" and the diagram label shows "Gear Data Record Type 7" as rendered visually in the PDF image, while the surrounding text says seven record types including "Record Type 8 Fishing Gear Data." HTML table reproduces "Record Type 8 | Fishing Gear Data" but the following plain-text diagram transcription says "Gear Data Record Type 7," creating inconsistency not cleanly resolved from the source and indicating table/diagram data integrity issues.

findings.description: Appendix B key-information table has at least one incorrect numeric value for the SPECIES CODE field position.
findings.evidence: PDF table under "Format and Position of Key Information Fields in the Database" shows "SPECIES CODE" with "Number of bytes 10" and position "11- 20". HTML renders "SPECIES CODE | 10 | 11- 12," truncating the end position and changing the table data.

findings.description: Appendix C data dictionary table does not faithfully preserve some original coded values and symbols.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the "Bottom Composition" comments list letter-coded material types such as "A = Dome", "B = Cube Units", "C = Pipe Units", etc. The HTML keeps the row limit as "1-9" and reproduces the comments with letters, suggesting mixed or inconsistent coding. Also PDF temperature units appear as degree-C symbols, while HTML shows "EC" and "☐C" for Air/Surface/Bottom Temperature, indicating OCR corruption of table cell content.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not faithful to the document structure: the main document title is split into multiple h3 elements and no single top-level heading is used.
findings.evidence: PDF title block reads as a centered document title: "Division of Marine Fisheries / CRFL Funding Data Delivery Specifications / January 2012." HTML encodes these as three separate <h3> elements rather than a single title heading, while section labels like "Purpose:" and "Background:" are plain paragraphs with bold text instead of heading tags.

findings.description: Several textual details are inaccurate due to OCR/conversion errors, altering meaning or precision.
findings.evidence: HTML says ".cvs" in "delimited text (.txt or .cvs) file" whereas the PDF shows ".txt or .cvs" visually, but elsewhere table text is clearly corrupted: HTML has "spstaus" instead of PDF's "spstaus"/"spstatus"-style field label as shown in Appendix A header, "which present in both records" instead of PDF "which is present in both records," and "Record Type II☐s" instead of the PDF's intended "Record Type II's" in Appendix C comments.
